article_title: Korean Researchers Develop Two-Dimensional Catalyst That Pushes Lithium-Air Batteries Past 550 Stable Cycles

content_preview.title: Korean Researchers Develop Two-Dimensional Catalyst That Pushes Lithium-Air Batteries Past 550 Stable Cycles
content_preview.summary: A KIST-IAE team engineered a tungsten diselenide catalyst that achieved over 550 stable cycles in lithium-air batteries, outperforming conventional catalysts and advancing a technology with ten times the energy density of lithium-ion.
content_preview.body_excerpt: A joint research team from the Korea Institute of Science and Technology (KIST) and the Institute for Advanced Engineering (IAE) has developed a two-dimensional catalyst that breaks through longstanding performance barriers in lithium-air batteries, according to a [study published in Materials Science and Engineering R: Reports](https://www.eurekalert.org/news-releases/1121822). The catalyst, based on tungsten diselenide (WSe₂) modified through atomic-level defect engineering, demonstrated stabl...

editor_notes.content_quality: Focused battery research article with clear technical detail.
editor_notes.source_verification: EurekAlert and Interesting Engineering sources confirmed reachable by automated check. Manual fetch limited by login requirements and URL changes. Article content consistent with press release patterns for KIST research.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Claims about WSe2 catalyst and cycle stability are technically plausible and consistent with recent battery research publications.
concerns: Manual source verification limited — EurekAlert requires login, IE returned 404. Automated checks passed.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Good battery research article. Approved based on automated checks and source credibility.
